text:
Market today: The mood is mixed. While strong AI chip demand is noted, ASML's stock falling after hiking its sales forecast is a concerning signal for the chip sector. This suggests underlying caution or profit-taking despite positive fundamentals, which directly impacts your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL is a "SELL" at -36.42% and SPAL is "NEW" at -38.17%. Both are leveraged ETFs with significant drawdowns, amplified by your margin trading. PTNM is a tiny spec at -15.25% with data issues.

Watch: Keep a close eye on how other major chip names react to the ASML news. Also, monitor the broader tech sector for any further signs of valuation shifts following the SpaceX news.

Bottom line: Focus on managing risk in your leveraged, underperforming positions as chip sector signals are mixed.
plays: Review SOXL's stop at 200. Its "SELL" status and deep -36.42% loss in a leveraged product demand immediate attention.
Re-evaluate SPAL's stop at 21.38. This new, leveraged ETF is down -38.17%, and margin amplifies this risk.
No new buys today - hold and watch for clearer signals in the chip sector, especially after ASML's unexpected drop.
